sync_policy.synchronization_rule: Keep ticket status aligned with canonical lifecycle progress whenever safe and possible. Ticket artifacts describe planned implementation work, but runtime transaction state and canonical event history remain authoritative for execution and resume decisions.

planning_focus.theme: Redesign the work loop as a resumable transactional protocol for ticket completion.
planning_focus.problem_statement: The core 0.6.0 problem is not only identifier ambiguity. The deeper issue is that the work loop itself must be specified as a durable, resumable transaction protocol so an interrupted MCP server session can still deterministically finish the active ticket.
planning_focus.transaction_definition: A transaction is one durable execution attempt for one ticket. It is the canonical unit of resumable work, distinct from planning identity and distinct from repository commit or terminal completion.
design_priorities: single active non-terminal transaction per workspace
append-only event history as rebuild source
commit remains non-terminal until explicit end
exact transaction continuation without heuristic replacement

tickets.title: Define the canonical resumable work transaction model
Define the canonical state machine and continuation rules
Define persistence, rebuild, and issuance responsibilities
Implement issued transaction IDs in place of ticket-derived tx_id generation
Remove sentinel active-transaction semantics from normal flow
Simplify resume logic to continue the exact active transaction
Bound session context and define historical compatibility behavior
Add first-resume regression coverage for interruption at transaction checkpoints
Add repeated-resume and idempotent continuation coverage for verify, commit, and terminal flows
Update documentation and operator guidance for the redesigned protocol

phase_summary.goal: Define the resumable transaction protocol, state machine, and persistence contract before runtime implementation changes.
Implement runtime identity and recovery simplifications so the active transaction can be resumed deterministically.
Harden the redesigned protocol with first-resume checkpoint coverage, repeated-resume idempotence coverage, and updated guidance.

status_context.reason: Phase 1 has completed the transaction model, state-machine definition, and persistence contract work. The next focus is Phase 2 implementation, starting with issued transaction IDs and removal of ticket-derived transaction identity assumptions.
status_context.recommended_action: Start p2-t01 to implement issued transaction IDs in place of ticket-derived `tx_id` generation, using the Phase 1 contracts as the authoritative boundary for identity, continuation, and persistence behavior.
